itineraries.name: Cote de Nuits Grand Cru two days
itineraries.theme: The red-wine heart of Burgundy: Gevrey-Chambertin, Chambolle-Musigny and Vosne-Romanee

days.morning: Drive south from Dijon along the Route des Grands Crus to Gevrey-Chambertin for a mid-morning visit at Domaine Trapet Pere et Fils, focused on the biodynamic Chambertin holdings.
days.lunch: Lunch in Gevrey-Chambertin village before continuing along the cru slope.
days.afternoon: Continue to Chambolle-Musigny for an afternoon appointment at Domaine Georges Roumier, the village reference for Bonnes-Mares and Musigny.
days.evening: Dinner and an evening glass in Beaune at Caves Madeleine on the Faubourg Madeleine.

days.morning: Drive to Vosne-Romanee, the most concentrated Grand Cru village in the world, for a morning visit at Domaine Meo-Camuzet.
days.lunch: Picnic among the vines or lunch in Nuits-Saint-Georges.
days.afternoon: Afternoon appointment at Domaine Faiveley in Nuits-Saint-Georges, a large family house with broad Cote de Nuits holdings.
days.evening: Return to Beaune for an evening tasting at the Cite des Climats before dinner.

itineraries.description: A two-day Cote de Nuits route from Gevrey-Chambertin through Chambolle-Musigny to Vosne-Romanee and Nuits-Saint-Georges, anchored by grower visits and Beaune evenings.
itineraries.tip: The top Cote de Nuits domaines book out months ahead and many accept visitors only by referral; line up appointments well before travelling.

itineraries.name: Cote de Beaune white-wine three days
itineraries.theme: Great white Burgundy: Beaune, Meursault and Puligny-Montrachet

days.morning: Begin in Beaune with a morning at Maison Joseph Drouhin, exploring the negociant-domaine's cellars beneath the old town.
days.lunch: Lunch in Beaune near the Hospices.
days.afternoon: Afternoon visit at Bouchard Pere et Fils in the Chateau de Beaune, with its historic cellar of old vintages.
days.evening: Evening glass at La Dilettante on the Faubourg Bretonniere.

days.morning: Drive south to Meursault for a morning at Domaine des Comtes Lafon, a benchmark white-wine estate.
days.afternoon: Continue to Puligny-Montrachet for an afternoon at Domaine Leflaive, the biodynamic reference for grand white Burgundy.
days.evening: Dinner in Beaune; nightcap at the bar of Hotel Le Cep.

days.morning: Visit Olivier Leflaive in Puligny-Montrachet, which pairs cellar tastings with a long lunch built around the estate's whites.
days.afternoon: Round out the trip at the Beaune Cite des Climats with a themed white-Burgundy tasting workshop.
days.evening: Final evening glass at La Maison du Colombier near the Hospices.

itineraries.description: A three-day Cote de Beaune route built on white Burgundy, from Beaune's negociant cellars to Meursault and Puligny-Montrachet, with Beaune evenings.
itineraries.tip: Olivier Leflaive's tasting-lunch is the most accessible way to drink serious Puligny without a private appointment; book it ahead.

itineraries.name: Chablis and northern Burgundy two days
itineraries.theme: Cool-climate Chardonnay: the Chablis Grands Crus and the Grand Auxerrois

days.morning: Start in Chablis with a morning at Domaine William Fevre, a leading owner of Grand Cru parcels on the famous slope.
days.lunch: Lunch in the town of Chablis.
days.afternoon: Afternoon at Domaine Louis Michel et Fils, known for unoaked, steely Chablis that shows the Kimmeridgian limestone clearly.
days.evening: Evening tasting at the Chablis site of the Cite des Climats comparing Petit Chablis up to Grand Cru.

days.morning: Visit Domaine Jean-Marc Brocard for a contrast of village, Premier and Grand Cru bottlings across the wider Chablis zone.
days.lunch: Lunch in Chablis before the drive south.
days.afternoon: Walk the Grand Cru slope above town (Les Clos, Vaudesir, Valmur) to see the seven climats on a single hillside.
days.evening: Dinner in Chablis with a Premier Cru pour.

itineraries.description: A two-day Chablis route through the Grand Cru slope and the Grand Auxerrois, built on cool-climate Chardonnay from oaked to steely styles.
itineraries.tip: Taste oaked and unoaked Chablis side by side; the contrast between William Fevre's barrel-influenced and Louis Michel's tank-raised wines defines the appellation's two schools.

itineraries.name: Cote Chalonnaise value tour two days
itineraries.theme: Affordable Burgundy: Rully, Mercurey, Givry and Bouzeron with a southern detour

days.morning: Drive south to the Cote Chalonnaise for a morning at Domaines Devillard, with holdings across Mercurey and the wider Chalonnaise.
days.afternoon: Afternoon discovery of Givry and Rully on foot and by car, the value heart of red and white Burgundy.
days.evening: Return north for an evening glass in Beaune at Le Bout du Monde.

days.morning: Continue south into the Maconnais for a morning at Maison Verget, a respected white-wine negociant working Macon and Pouilly-Fuisse fruit.
days.lunch: Lunch beneath the Roche de Solutre.
days.afternoon: Taste Pouilly-Fuisse and Saint-Veran in the southern villages before the drive back north.
days.evening: Final dinner and a Cremant nightcap in Beaune.

itineraries.description: A two-day value tour through the Cote Chalonnaise and Maconnais, from Mercurey and Givry to Pouilly-Fuisse, showing affordable Burgundy at its best.
itineraries.tip: This is the trip for drinkers who love Burgundy but balk at Cote d'Or prices; the Chalonnaise and Maconnais deliver the grapes and the terroir for far less.
